entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Cultivated peanut (Arachis hypogaea L.), an important oil and protein crop

entities.name: RECOMBINATION HOT SPOTS
entities.description: High density regions of crossover events in the genome

entities.name: MEIOSIS
entities.description: The cell division process that causes recombination hot spots

entities.name: RECOMBINATION EVENTS
entities.description: Genetic exchange events occurring during meiosis

entities.name: SLAF MARKERS
entities.description: Sequence-based markers used for genome-wide identification of recombination hot spots

entities.name: RIL POPULATION
entities.description: Recombinant Inbred Line population used to evaluate recombination events

entities.name: NATURAL POPULATION
entities.description: A population of 49 cultivars used to evaluate recombination events

entities.name: CROSSOVER EVENTS
entities.description: Specific recombination events detected per plant

entities.name: B SUB-GENOME
entities.description: One of the two sub-genomes in peanut showing specific crossover event distribution

entities.name: A SUB-GENOME
entities.description: One of the two sub-genomes in peanut showing specific crossover event distribution

entities.name: CHROMOSOMES
entities.description: Structures containing the genome where crossover events are distributed

entities.name: MARKER HAPLOTYPES CONSERVATIVE REGION
entities.description: Genomic regions that show a negative relationship with recombination hot spots

entities.name: HEATMAP OF RECOMBINATION
entities.description: A visualization tool showing a positive relationship with recombination hot spots

entities.name: GO CATEGORIES
entities.description: Gene Ontology categories used to analyze the function of genes in RHP regions

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL STIMULI
entities.description: External factors that trigger responses from genes located in RHP regions

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The process of peanut adjusting to changing environments, facilitated by recombination
